info.name: Bifrost Provider Harness
info.description:
Smoke-test harness exercising every Bifrost provider surface.

Covers:
1. Native Bifrost API (`/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/responses`).
2. Drop-in integrations: `/openai`, `/anthropic`, `/bedrock`, `/genai`, plus Azure (via `/openai/openai/deployments/...`) and Vertex (via `/genai`).
3. Criss-Cross matrix (§8): every drop-in request shape × every model provider × every supported modality (chat, streaming, embeddings, audio, image gen, tool calling, vision, JSON, reasoning). Cells where the provider doesn't support the modality are tagged `[SKIP]` and skipped at runtime unless `include_skip=1`.

Set the `baseUrl` and provider key variables before running. Run end-to-end with `make run-provider-harness-test` from the repo root. Run criss-cross only with `make run-provider-harness-test FOLDER="8. Criss-Cross"`.

exec: // Skip [PREVIEW]-tagged requests unless include_preview=1 (set via Newman --env-var include_preview=1).
// Use for account/region-scoped resources: preview models, vector stores, cached content, MCP servers.
// Skip [SKIP]-tagged requests unless include_skip=1 (set via Newman --env-var include_skip=1).
// Used in the criss-cross matrix (§8) to mark provider+modality cells that return NewUnsupportedOperationError by design (e.g., anthropic embeddings, bedrock audio).
// Per-request compat override: when compat is set (via Newman --env-var compat=true), inject the x-bf-compat header on every request.
// Absent => harness baseline (compat config off). Lets the suite run once with compat off and once with compat on.
// Prompt-caching harness (Round 31): stable per-run nonce so write/read pairs share a cold-then-warm prefix.
// Only register the content-shape test when the request actually succeeded.
// Otherwise newman reports it as passing (because of an early return) which is misleading next to a failing status.
pm.test('Response has content (text or tool_use)', function () {
if (first > 0 && first < 0x20 && first !== 0x09 && first !== 0x0a && first !== 0x0d) { return; }
pm.expect.fail('response was not valid JSON: ' + e.message);
// Stop-sequence truncation can yield content:null with non-zero completion_tokens — treat as valid response.
if (!hasContent && j.choices[0].finish_reason === 'stop' && j.usage && j.usage.completion_tokens > 0) { hasContent = true; }
hasContent = j.content.some(function (c) { return c && (c.type === 'text' && c.text) || c.type === 'tool_use' || c.type === 'server_tool_use'; });
hasContent = parts.some(function (p) { return p && (p.text || p.functionCall || p.executableCode); });
hasContent = j.output.some(function (o) { return o && (o.type === 'message' || o.type === 'tool_call' || o.type === 'function_call' || (o.content && o.content.length)); });
pm.expect(hasContent, 'expected non-empty content (shape=' + shape + ', body=' + JSON.stringify(j).slice(0, 200) + ')').to.be.true;

item.name: 1. Native Bifrost API
item.description: Bifrost's own OpenAI-shaped surface. Use a `provider/model` string in the `model` field to route to any configured backend.

item.name: 2. OpenAI drop-in (/openai)
item.description: Mirrors the OpenAI HTTP surface. Existing OpenAI SDKs work by pointing `base_url` at `{{baseUrl}}/openai`.

item.name: 3. Anthropic drop-in (/anthropic)
item.description: Mirrors the Anthropic Messages API. Use `x-api-key` and `anthropic-version` headers as you would against api.anthropic.com.

item.name: 4. Bedrock drop-in (/bedrock)
item.description: Mirrors AWS Bedrock's Converse API. Auth/credentials are resolved by Bifrost from its provider config (or IAM/env if Bedrock provider config is nil).

pm.expect(Array.isArray(oa), 'outputAssessments must not be an array').to.be.false;
// Bedrock native streaming (converse-stream). Verifies the upstream is delivered
// as a live AWS event stream with multiple incremental frames, guarding against the
// server-side gzip buffering regression where all events arrive at once (issue #4542).
pm.test('Bedrock stream is an AWS event stream', function () {
pm.test('Bedrock stream contains multiple incremental events', function () {
pm.expect(body, 'expected a messageStop event terminating the stream').to.include('messageStop');
pm.test('Bedrock converse-stream closes content blocks (contentBlockStop before messageStop) - #4923', function () {
pm.expect(b2, 'expected a contentBlockStop event closing each content block').to.include('contentBlockStop');
// Bedrock streaming via the OpenAI-compatible SSE path (/v1/chat/completions).
// Confirms chunked SSE delivery with multiple incremental content deltas and a
// [DONE] terminator. Note: newman buffers the full response, so this validates
// SSE framing/chunking, not wall-clock TTFB (that is covered by the Go unit test
pm.test('Bedrock SSE: content-type is text/event-stream', function () {
pm.test('Bedrock SSE: multiple data chunks with incremental content deltas', function () {
var chunks = 0, sawDelta = false, sawDone = false;
try { var j = JSON.parse(p); var d = j.choices && j.choices[0] && j.choices[0].delta; if (d && typeof d.content === 'string' && d.content.length) { sawDelta = true; } } catch (e) {}
pm.expect(chunks, 'expected > 1 SSE data chunks, got ' + chunks).to.be.above(1);
pm.expect(sawDelta, 'expected at least one chunk with an incremental content delta').to.be.true;

// Confirms chunked SSE delivery with multiple incremental content deltas and a
// [DONE] terminator. Note: newman buffers the full response, so this validates
// SSE framing/chunking, not wall-clock TTFB (that is covered by the Go unit test
pm.test('Bedrock SSE: content-type is text/event-stream', function () {
pm.test('Bedrock SSE: multiple data chunks with incremental content deltas', function () {
var chunks = 0, sawDelta = false, sawDone = false;
try { var j = JSON.parse(p); var d = j.choices && j.choices[0] && j.choices[0].delta; if (d && typeof d.content === 'string' && d.content.length) { sawDelta = true; } } catch (e) {}
pm.expect(chunks, 'expected > 1 SSE data chunks, got ' + chunks).to.be.above(1);
pm.expect(sawDelta, 'expected at least one chunk with an incremental content delta').to.be.true;

item.name: 5. Azure (via /openai deployments)
item.description: Azure OpenAI has no separate top-level route - it's served by the OpenAI integration with the deployment-style path. Replace `{{azureDeployment}}` with the deployment name configured in your Azure resource.

item.name: 6. Google GenAI drop-in (/genai)
item.description: Mirrors Google GenAI's `generateContent` API. Send the model + action via the `:generateContent` suffix on the path.

item.name: 7. Vertex (via /genai)
item.description: Bifrost surfaces Vertex AI through the same GenAI router; use a Vertex-style model identifier (or rely on Bifrost's provider routing in your config) and the `generateContent` path.

item.name: 8. Criss-Cross (endpoint × provider × modality matrix)
item.description:
Every Bifrost drop-in request shape × every model provider × every supported modality.

Bifrost's `ParseModelString` (core/schemas/utils.go) splits the `provider/model-name` prefix and dispatches to the right backend; per-shape converters in `transports/bifrost-http/integrations/` normalize the incoming payload to `BifrostChatRequest` (or the equivalent for embeddings/audio/images) regardless of which provider serves it.

Sub-folders:
- 8.1 Text Chat (non-streaming) — rows A-G × providers
- 8.2 Text Chat (streaming) — same matrix with stream:true / :streamGenerateContent / converse-stream
- 8.3 Embeddings — rows H-J × providers
- 8.4 Audio — transcriptions (K-L) + speech (M-N)
- 8.5 Image generation / editing — rows O-Q
- 8.6 Feature combinations — tool calling, vision, JSON/structured output, reasoning

Cells where the provider doesn't support the modality (e.g., Anthropic embeddings, Bedrock audio input — see core/providers/*/{anthropic.go:1878-1923, bedrock.go:1936-1941}) are tagged `[SKIP]` and skipped at runtime unless `include_skip=1`.

item.name: 8.1 Text Chat (non-streaming)
item.description: Minimal `Hello` text-chat request across every endpoint shape × every model provider. Native /v1/chat/completions is the diagonal (provider/model strings only); rows B-G exercise the cross-shape conversion path (e.g., /anthropic/v1/messages with model=openai/gpt-5).

item.name: 8.1.A Native /v1/chat/completions (unified, 10 per provider)
item.description: The headline demo: one endpoint, one request shape, only the `model` field varies. 5 providers x 10 models = 50 requests (Azure intentionally skipped — Azure deployments use a different URL shape, covered separately in 8.1.C and the legacy §5).

item.name: 8.1.B Native /v1/responses (Responses shape × all providers)
item.description: OpenAI Responses-API request shape (`input` instead of `messages`) against the native endpoint with model strings from all 6 providers. Bifrost's Responses-shape converter normalizes to BifrostChatRequest internally; the same converter must handle every backend.

item.name: 8.1.C OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/chat/completions × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI ChatCompletion shape via the `/openai` drop-in prefix, routed to non-OpenAI backends via `provider/` model prefix. The OpenAI diagonal is already in §2 and 8.1.A; here we exercise the drop-in's converter against backends that need shape translation.

item.name: 8.1.D OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/responses × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI Responses-API shape via `/openai` drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI backends. Responses-shape converter must round-trip through every provider's chat backend.

item.name: 8.1.E Anthropic drop-in /anthropic/v1/messages × non-Anthropic providers
item.description: Anthropic Messages-API shape (requires `max_tokens`) routed to non-Anthropic backends. Tests Bifrost's Messages→BifrostChatRequest converter against every other provider.

item.name: 8.1.F GenAI drop-in /genai/v1beta/models/{model}:generateContent × non-Gemini providers
item.description: Google GenAI generateContent shape (`contents` / `parts` body, model in URL path) routed to non-Gemini backends. The provider/model prefix is part of the path; Bifrost's GenAI router (`integrations/router.go`) parses it before invoking the backend.

item.name: 8.2 Text Chat (streaming)
item.description:
Streaming variants of the 8.1 matrix. OpenAI / Anthropic / Responses shapes use `stream: true` in the body; GenAI uses `:streamGenerateContent?alt=sse`; Bedrock uses the `/converse-stream` URL.

The folder-level test script (inherited by all items) asserts the Content-Type is a streaming type and that the body contains a recognized stream terminator (`[DONE]` for OpenAI SSE, `message_stop` / `message_delta` for Anthropic, `finishReason` for GenAI, `messageStop` for Bedrock).

exec: // Streaming-specific assertion: header + full body consume + terminator check.
// Runs in addition to the collection-level 2xx check. The collection-level content-shape test skips on streaming responses (see line ~34), so this fills the gap.
pm.expect(hit, 'no recognized stream terminator. First 200 chars: ' + body.slice(0, 200)).to.be.true;

item.name: 8.2.A Native /v1/chat/completions streaming × all providers
item.description: Streaming chat completions via the unified native endpoint, one model per provider.

item.name: 8.2.B Native /v1/responses streaming × all providers
item.description: Streaming Responses-API via native endpoint.

item.name: 8.2.C OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/chat/completions streaming × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI streaming chat shape via drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI backends.

item.name: 8.2.D OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/responses streaming × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI streaming Responses shape via drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI backends.

item.name: 8.2.E Anthropic drop-in /anthropic/v1/messages streaming × non-Anthropic providers
item.description: Anthropic streaming Messages-API shape via drop-in, routed to non-Anthropic backends.

item.name: 8.2.F GenAI drop-in :streamGenerateContent × non-Gemini providers
item.description: Google GenAI streaming via `:streamGenerateContent?alt=sse`, routed to non-Gemini backends. Body unchanged from non-streaming generateContent; SSE is requested via the URL suffix + query.

item.name: 8.3 Embeddings
item.description:
Embedding endpoints × providers. Anthropic returns NewUnsupportedOperationError for Embedding (core/providers/anthropic/anthropic.go:1878-1923) — those cells are tagged `[SKIP]` and skipped unless `include_skip=1`.

Response shapes (validated by extended top-level detector):
- OpenAI: `{data: [{embedding: [...]}]}` → list-data branch
- GenAI: `{embedding: {values: [...]}}` → genai-embedding branch
- Bedrock Titan: `{embedding: [...]}` → bedrock-titan-embedding branch
- Vertex: `{predictions: [{embeddings: {values}}]}` → vertex-predictions branch

item.name: 8.3.H Native /v1/embeddings × all providers
item.description: Native embeddings endpoint with model strings from every provider. OpenAI-shape request, but Bifrost routes to the model's actual provider.

item.name: 8.3.I OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/embeddings × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI Embeddings shape via drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI backends.

item.name: 8.3.J GenAI drop-in /genai/v1beta/models/{model}:embedContent × non-Gemini providers
item.description: Google GenAI embedContent shape (`content.parts`), model in URL path. Routed to non-Gemini embedding backends.

item.name: 8.4 Audio (transcription + speech)
item.description:
Audio endpoints × providers. Most providers don't expose a dedicated audio endpoint through Bifrost — those cells are `[SKIP]`:
- Anthropic: no audio (core/providers/anthropic/anthropic.go:1878-1923)
- Vertex: no transcription (core/providers/vertex/vertex.go:1722-1729)
- Bedrock: no transcription (core/providers/bedrock/bedrock.go:1936-1941); audio input rejected (core/providers/bedrock/utils.go:1058)
- Gemini/Vertex TTS: no /v1/audio/speech equivalent

Transcription tests require `tests/e2e/api/fixtures/sample.mp3` (small audio sample) at runtime — see Phase 7 verification.

Response shapes (validated by extended top-level detector):
- Transcription JSON: `{text: "..."}` → audio-transcription branch
- TTS binary: `audio/*` Content-Type → prelude's non-JSON early-return passes status-only check

item.name: 8.4.K Native /v1/audio/transcriptions × providers
item.description: Transcription endpoint with multipart formdata. Supported providers: OpenAI (Whisper, gpt-4o-mini-transcribe), Gemini (via generateContent under the hood), Azure (Whisper deployment).

item.name: 8.4.L OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/audio/transcriptions × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI Transcriptions shape via drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI transcription backends.

item.name: 8.4.M Native /v1/audio/speech × providers
item.description: Text-to-speech endpoint. Supported: OpenAI, Azure. Anthropic/Gemini/Vertex/Bedrock have no `/v1/audio/speech`-equivalent endpoint in Bifrost.

item.name: 8.4.N OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/audio/speech × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI TTS shape via drop-in. Only Azure has a comparable backend.

item.name: 8.5 Image generation
item.description:
Image generation endpoints × providers. Anthropic returns NewUnsupportedOperationError for ImageGeneration (core/providers/anthropic/anthropic.go:1878-1923) — those cells are `[SKIP]`.

Response shapes (validated by extended top-level detector):
- OpenAI: `{data: [{url|b64_json}]}` → list-data branch
- Vertex Imagen: `{predictions: [{bytesBase64Encoded}]}` → vertex-predictions branch
- Bedrock Titan/Nova: `{images: ["base64"]}` → bedrock-images branch

Omits /v1/images/edits — needs a binary PNG fixture; will be added later if needed.

item.name: 8.5.O Native /v1/images/generations × providers
item.description: Image generation endpoint with model strings from all providers. OpenAI request shape; Bifrost translates to backend-specific format internally.

item.name: 8.5.P OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/images/generations × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI image-generation shape via drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI backends.

item.name: 8.6 Feature combinations
item.description:
Feature-specific cross-shape tests: tool calling, vision (image input), JSON / structured output, reasoning / thinking.

Each feature is tested across multiple endpoint shapes with multiple model providers, proving Bifrost's converters preserve feature semantics across shape boundaries (e.g., OpenAI `tools` array → Anthropic `tools` shape when routed to Claude; Anthropic `tools` shape → Gemini `function_declarations` when routed to Gemini).

Response-shape assertions (`tool_calls` / `tool_use` / `functionCall`) are already in the top-level detector.

item.name: 8.6.1 Tool calling × endpoint shapes × providers
item.description: Function-calling request. Each shape uses its native tool schema (OpenAI `tools[].function`, Anthropic `tools[].input_schema`, GenAI `tools[].function_declarations`, Bedrock `toolConfig.tools[].toolSpec`). The converter must translate to the backend's native tool shape and translate the response back.

item.name: 8.6.2 Vision (image input) × endpoint shapes × providers
item.description:
Vision request with a 1×1 transparent PNG (base64 inline). Each shape uses its native image-content schema:
- OpenAI: `content: [{type:"image_url", image_url:{url:"data:image/png;base64,..."}}]`
- Anthropic: `content: [{type:"image", source:{type:"base64", media_type, data}}]`
- GenAI: `parts: [{inline_data:{mime_type, data}}]`
- Bedrock: `content: [{image:{format, source:{bytes}}}]`

item.name: 8.6.3 JSON / structured output × providers
item.description: OpenAI `response_format: {type: "json_schema", json_schema: {...}}` via native chat endpoint, routed to each provider's structured-output mechanism (Anthropic tool-use forcing, Gemini responseSchema, Bedrock tool-spec).

item.name: 8.6.4 Reasoning / thinking × providers
item.description:
Reasoning/thinking activation via the request field appropriate for the model's native provider:
- OpenAI o-series: `reasoning_effort: "low"`
- Anthropic: `thinking: {type: "enabled", budget_tokens: 1024}`
- Gemini 2.5+: `thinkingConfig: {thinkingBudget: 1024}` (or via Bifrost's OpenAI-shape `reasoning_effort` translation)

Bifrost's converter maps each field to the backend's native reasoning parameter.

item.name: 8.6.5 xAI x_search (server-side tool)
item.description:
xAI-native x_search tool tests via the native /v1/responses endpoint.

The x_search tool is a server-side search tool exclusive to xAI (grok models). When invoked the model internally calls x_semantic_search and/or x_keyword_search, returning custom_tool_call output items. The final message output_text content block carries url_citation annotations pointing at the source tweets/posts.

Docs: https://docs.x.ai/developers/tools/x-search

Covered here:
  8.6.5.A – basic x_search, no extra params → verifies custom_tool_call items + final message
  8.6.5.B – x_search with allowed_x_handles filter → tool_choice=required, verifies sub-tool name
  8.6.5.C – x_search with from_date/to_date → date-range filtering passes through
  8.6.5.D – x_search with all optional params (exact bug-report repro)
  8.6.5.E – x_search streaming → custom_tool_call_input events flow through the SSE stream
  8.6.5.F – x_search with url_citation annotations → verifies annotations array in output_text block

item.name: 9. Passthrough (catch-all forwarding)
item.description: Catch-all routes that forward the request body byte-for-byte to the upstream provider, with Bifrost injecting its own configured key. The handler at transports/bifrost-http/integrations/router.go:604 strips Authorization / API-Key / X-API-Key / X-Goog-API-Key from the inbound request - so these requests intentionally omit those headers. Bedrock and Vertex have no passthrough variant (AWS SigV4 / Google OAuth can't be bridged this way).

item.name: MCP Tool Handling cross-cut
item.description: Regression #3795: a provider-side `type:"mcp"` server tool in a Responses request must be silently dropped for providers without an MCP connector (Bedrock, Vertex), not rejected. Function tools survive the strip. Function-bearing rows force the surviving function tool via tool_choice and verify it is invoked (output function_call / streamed response.function_call_arguments). Lone-mcp rows exercise the all-tools-dropped edge case: zero tools left after the strip must still return a text answer with NO tool call. Server/MCP tool calls are NOT expected for Bedrock/Vertex Claude — that is correct. Covers native + /openai drop-in + streaming.

item.name: bedrock/global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7 · tool_choice pins absent fn + lone mcp dropped → reconciled, no 400 (PR #4573)

exec: // Fix (PR #4573, CodeRabbit): tool_choice pins a function, but the ONLY tool sent is an
// unsupported mcp server tool. The mcp tool is dropped -> zero tools, so the dangling
// pin must be reconciled away (fall back to Bedrock 'auto') instead of emitting a
// toolChoice.tool that references an absent tool, which Bedrock rejects with HTTP 400.
// The collection-level 'Status code is 2xx' is the real regression guard (pre-fix => 400).
pm.test('mcp dropped + dangling tool_choice reconciled, no 400 (PR #4573)', function () {
pm.expect(raw.toLowerCase(), 'pinned tool must not leak to Bedrock as an unknown tool').to.not.include('tool not found');
pm.test('no tool call (pin reconciled away, zero tools left)', function () {
var calls = out.filter(function (o) { return o && (o.type === 'function_call' || o.type === 'tool_call'); });

item.name: Cross-Cut Round 30: Opus 4.8 Feature Gating
item.description:
Exercises the Anthropic request-surface features on Claude Opus 4.8, the live successor after Fable 5 / Mythos were discontinued (https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access).
Surface covered: adaptive thinking, budget_tokens to adaptive, sampling-param handling (temperature/top_p/top_k), effort high/xhigh/max, structured outputs, task budgets, computer-use new-gen tools, web_search dynamic filtering, mid-conversation system messages, and fast mode.
Scripts are guarded on code<400 so the suite tolerates accounts without access to a given feature (same convention as the [PREVIEW] items).

item.name: 13. OpenRouter Prompt Caching (cache_control)
item.description: Sends OpenAI-format chat requests through the OpenRouter provider with Anthropic-style cache_control breakpoints on content blocks and tools. OpenRouter forwards cache_control to Claude/Gemini (explicit caching) and harmlessly ignores it for implicit-caching models (OpenAI/DeepSeek) — these must NOT 400. Bifrost preserves cache_control only when the provider is OpenRouter (stripped for native OpenAI).

exec: if (pm.response.code < 400) { pm.test('cache_control forwarded: response ok + usage present', function () { var j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.usage || {}).to.be.an('object'); }); } pm.test('cache_control not rejected (no 400)', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.equal(400); });

item.name: 14. No-Prefix Integration Routes (provider inference)
item.description: Integration drop-in requests (/openai, /anthropic, /genai, /bedrock, /cohere, /langchain, /litellm, /pydanticai, /cursor) whose model carried a known Bifrost provider prefix, with that prefix removed from the body "model" field and the /models/<provider>/ URL segment. Exercises provider inference when callers omit the provider prefix. Native /v1/* and *_passthrough routes are excluded. Run with: make run-provider-harness-no-prefix-test

item.name: 8. Criss-Cross (endpoint × provider × modality matrix)
item.description:
Every Bifrost drop-in request shape × every model provider × every supported modality.

Bifrost's `ParseModelString` (core/schemas/utils.go) splits the `provider/model-name` prefix and dispatches to the right backend; per-shape converters in `transports/bifrost-http/integrations/` normalize the incoming payload to `BifrostChatRequest` (or the equivalent for embeddings/audio/images) regardless of which provider serves it.

Sub-folders:
- 8.1 Text Chat (non-streaming) — rows A-G × providers
- 8.2 Text Chat (streaming) — same matrix with stream:true / :streamGenerateContent / converse-stream
- 8.3 Embeddings — rows H-J × providers
- 8.4 Audio — transcriptions (K-L) + speech (M-N)
- 8.5 Image generation / editing — rows O-Q
- 8.6 Feature combinations — tool calling, vision, JSON/structured output, reasoning

Cells where the provider doesn't support the modality (e.g., Anthropic embeddings, Bedrock audio input — see core/providers/*/{anthropic.go:1878-1923, bedrock.go:1936-1941}) are tagged `[SKIP]` and skipped at runtime unless `include_skip=1`.

item.name: 8.1 Text Chat (non-streaming)
item.description: Minimal `Hello` text-chat request across every endpoint shape × every model provider. Native /v1/chat/completions is the diagonal (provider/model strings only); rows B-G exercise the cross-shape conversion path (e.g., /anthropic/v1/messages with model=openai/gpt-5).

item.name: 8.1.C OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/chat/completions × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI ChatCompletion shape via the `/openai` drop-in prefix, routed to non-OpenAI backends via `provider/` model prefix. The OpenAI diagonal is already in §2 and 8.1.A; here we exercise the drop-in's converter against backends that need shape translation.

item.name: 8.1.D OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/responses × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI Responses-API shape via `/openai` drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI backends. Responses-shape converter must round-trip through every provider's chat backend.

item.name: 8.1.E Anthropic drop-in /anthropic/v1/messages × non-Anthropic providers
item.description: Anthropic Messages-API shape (requires `max_tokens`) routed to non-Anthropic backends. Tests Bifrost's Messages→BifrostChatRequest converter against every other provider.

item.name: 8.1.F GenAI drop-in /genai/v1beta/models/{model}:generateContent × non-Gemini providers
item.description: Google GenAI generateContent shape (`contents` / `parts` body, model in URL path) routed to non-Gemini backends. The provider/model prefix is part of the path; Bifrost's GenAI router (`integrations/router.go`) parses it before invoking the backend.

item.name: 8.2 Text Chat (streaming)
item.description:
Streaming variants of the 8.1 matrix. OpenAI / Anthropic / Responses shapes use `stream: true` in the body; GenAI uses `:streamGenerateContent?alt=sse`; Bedrock uses the `/converse-stream` URL.

The folder-level test script (inherited by all items) asserts the Content-Type is a streaming type and that the body contains a recognized stream terminator (`[DONE]` for OpenAI SSE, `message_stop` / `message_delta` for Anthropic, `finishReason` for GenAI, `messageStop` for Bedrock).

exec: // Streaming-specific assertion: header + full body consume + terminator check.
// Runs in addition to the collection-level 2xx check. The collection-level content-shape test skips on streaming responses (see line ~34), so this fills the gap.
pm.expect(hit, 'no recognized stream terminator. First 200 chars: ' + body.slice(0, 200)).to.be.true;

item.name: 8.2.C OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/chat/completions streaming × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI streaming chat shape via drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI backends.

item.name: 8.2.D OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/responses streaming × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI streaming Responses shape via drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI backends.

item.name: 8.2.E Anthropic drop-in /anthropic/v1/messages streaming × non-Anthropic providers
item.description: Anthropic streaming Messages-API shape via drop-in, routed to non-Anthropic backends.

item.name: 8.2.F GenAI drop-in :streamGenerateContent × non-Gemini providers
item.description: Google GenAI streaming via `:streamGenerateContent?alt=sse`, routed to non-Gemini backends. Body unchanged from non-streaming generateContent; SSE is requested via the URL suffix + query.

item.name: 8.3 Embeddings
item.description:
Embedding endpoints × providers. Anthropic returns NewUnsupportedOperationError for Embedding (core/providers/anthropic/anthropic.go:1878-1923) — those cells are tagged `[SKIP]` and skipped unless `include_skip=1`.

Response shapes (validated by extended top-level detector):
- OpenAI: `{data: [{embedding: [...]}]}` → list-data branch
- GenAI: `{embedding: {values: [...]}}` → genai-embedding branch
- Bedrock Titan: `{embedding: [...]}` → bedrock-titan-embedding branch
- Vertex: `{predictions: [{embeddings: {values}}]}` → vertex-predictions branch

item.name: 8.3.I OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/embeddings × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI Embeddings shape via drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI backends.

item.name: 8.3.J GenAI drop-in /genai/v1beta/models/{model}:embedContent × non-Gemini providers
item.description: Google GenAI embedContent shape (`content.parts`), model in URL path. Routed to non-Gemini embedding backends.

item.name: 8.4 Audio (transcription + speech)
item.description:
Audio endpoints × providers. Most providers don't expose a dedicated audio endpoint through Bifrost — those cells are `[SKIP]`:
- Anthropic: no audio (core/providers/anthropic/anthropic.go:1878-1923)
- Vertex: no transcription (core/providers/vertex/vertex.go:1722-1729)
- Bedrock: no transcription (core/providers/bedrock/bedrock.go:1936-1941); audio input rejected (core/providers/bedrock/utils.go:1058)
- Gemini/Vertex TTS: no /v1/audio/speech equivalent

Transcription tests require `tests/e2e/api/fixtures/sample.mp3` (small audio sample) at runtime — see Phase 7 verification.

Response shapes (validated by extended top-level detector):
- Transcription JSON: `{text: "..."}` → audio-transcription branch
- TTS binary: `audio/*` Content-Type → prelude's non-JSON early-return passes status-only check

item.name: 8.4.N OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/audio/speech × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI TTS shape via drop-in. Only Azure has a comparable backend.

item.name: 8.5 Image generation
item.description:
Image generation endpoints × providers. Anthropic returns NewUnsupportedOperationError for ImageGeneration (core/providers/anthropic/anthropic.go:1878-1923) — those cells are `[SKIP]`.

Response shapes (validated by extended top-level detector):
- OpenAI: `{data: [{url|b64_json}]}` → list-data branch
- Vertex Imagen: `{predictions: [{bytesBase64Encoded}]}` → vertex-predictions branch
- Bedrock Titan/Nova: `{images: ["base64"]}` → bedrock-images branch

Omits /v1/images/edits — needs a binary PNG fixture; will be added later if needed.

item.name: 8.5.P OpenAI drop-in /openai/v1/images/generations × non-OpenAI providers
item.description: OpenAI image-generation shape via drop-in, routed to non-OpenAI backends.

item.name: 8.6 Feature combinations
item.description:
Feature-specific cross-shape tests: tool calling, vision (image input), JSON / structured output, reasoning / thinking.

Each feature is tested across multiple endpoint shapes with multiple model providers, proving Bifrost's converters preserve feature semantics across shape boundaries (e.g., OpenAI `tools` array → Anthropic `tools` shape when routed to Claude; Anthropic `tools` shape → Gemini `function_declarations` when routed to Gemini).

Response-shape assertions (`tool_calls` / `tool_use` / `functionCall`) are already in the top-level detector.

item.name: 8.6.1 Tool calling × endpoint shapes × providers
item.description: Function-calling request. Each shape uses its native tool schema (OpenAI `tools[].function`, Anthropic `tools[].input_schema`, GenAI `tools[].function_declarations`, Bedrock `toolConfig.tools[].toolSpec`). The converter must translate to the backend's native tool shape and translate the response back.

item.name: 8.6.2 Vision (image input) × endpoint shapes × providers
item.description:
Vision request with a 1×1 transparent PNG (base64 inline). Each shape uses its native image-content schema:
- OpenAI: `content: [{type:"image_url", image_url:{url:"data:image/png;base64,..."}}]`
- Anthropic: `content: [{type:"image", source:{type:"base64", media_type, data}}]`
- GenAI: `parts: [{inline_data:{mime_type, data}}]`
- Bedrock: `content: [{image:{format, source:{bytes}}}]`

item.name: 8.6.4 Reasoning / thinking × providers
item.description:
Reasoning/thinking activation via the request field appropriate for the model's native provider:
- OpenAI o-series: `reasoning_effort: "low"`
- Anthropic: `thinking: {type: "enabled", budget_tokens: 1024}`
- Gemini 2.5+: `thinkingConfig: {thinkingBudget: 1024}` (or via Bifrost's OpenAI-shape `reasoning_effort` translation)

Bifrost's converter maps each field to the backend's native reasoning parameter.

item.name: 11. Cross-Provider Feature Tests
item.description: Same feature exercised across multiple providers via Bifrost's unified routing. Each sub-folder is one capability; each request differs only by `model` (or path) to show that Bifrost translates the request shape per-provider.

When the compat plugin converts /v1/chat/completions to the OpenAI Responses API, the replayed tool call becomes a function_call input item that must NOT carry role - OpenAI rejects role on any non-message input item with 400 unknown_parameter input[N].role. Cases 1-2 drive the real conversion route (x-bf-compat forces convert_chat_to_responses); case 3 pins the wire invariant on the native /v1/responses route with a caller-supplied role-bearing function_call item.

item.name: 20. Anthropic Redacted Thinking Round-Trip (#5093 / PR #5094)
item.description: Regression coverage for PR #5094 (fixes #5093): Anthropic returns safety-flagged reasoning as redacted_thinking blocks (an encrypted data payload, arriving complete in content_block_start with no deltas) and requires them to be replayed unmodified in multi-turn tool use - dropping them is the documented top cause of the 400 'thinking or redacted_thinking blocks in the latest assistant message cannot be modified'. Pre-fix, the responses surface lost the blocks twice: ToBifrostResponsesStream had no content_block_start case for redacted_thinking (streaming clients received zero encrypted_content items), and convertBifrostReasoningToAnthropicThinking gated on Summary != nil, so items carrying an empty summary list next to encrypted_content were never converted back on replay. Cases use Anthropic's documented magic string to deterministically trigger redacted thinking. Case 1 pins the streaming emission; case 2 pins the non-streaming baseline and captures the replay input into a collection variable; case 3 replays the tool-use continuation, which 400s pre-fix.

item.name: 21. Responses Stream Usage Persistence (#4846 / PR #4899)
item.description: Regression coverage for PR #4899 (fixes #4846): the final response.completed SSE event of a Responses stream carries response.usage, but the streaming accumulator dedupes chunks by ChunkIndex and reads stream metadata from the highest index. OpenAI-compatible Responses streams set ChunkIndex from the provider's sequence_number (core/providers/openai/openai.go), so providers that omit or reuse sequence_number (e.g. Unsloth via Codex CLI) delivered the terminal event with an already-seen index - it was deduped away and LLM Logs persisted 0/0 tokens with no raw JSON. The fix remaps late terminal events to a reserved trailing index. These cases pin the end-to-end invariant (usage on the wire implies usage in the logs DB) via the dbverify reporter (x-request-id + x-bf-expect-cost, asserting cost>0 && total_tokens>0), across the native OpenAI Responses SSE path and the converted Anthropic path, with a non-streaming baseline to isolate streaming-specific failures. Note: live OpenAI numbers its events correctly, so the exact omitted-sequence_number trigger is pinned by the Go unit test in PR #4899; a faithful harness repro would need an OpenAI-compatible mock provider in the gateway config.

item.name: 24. Responses Structured Output Schema Key Order (#5148 / PR #5151)
item.description: Issue #5148 / PR #5151: Bifrost alphabetized every nested object inside a Responses request's text.format.schema (schema fields were map[string]any and the outbound marshal uses SortMapKeys), violating OpenAI's documented Structured Outputs key-ordering contract. Under constrained decoding, schema key order steers anyOf branch selection: alphabetizing TextPart {type, text} into {text, type} makes the natural '{"type":' opener grammatically eliminate the TextPart branch, locking generation into the sibling WebCitation branch - schema-valid but fabricated output (0/5 correct pre-fix, 5/5 direct-to-OpenAI and post-fix). PR #5151 migrates the schema-bearing fields to OrderedMap. These cases send the issue's exact discriminated-union schema (each $defs member declares its 'type' discriminator before the payload field, deliberately non-alphabetical) and assert the model returns the requested TextPart, not a fabricated cite:web citation, across the native non-streaming, native streaming, and /openai drop-in decode paths.

item.name: 26. Anthropic Native Server-Side Fallback (fallbacks shape overload)
item.description:
Pins the overloaded request-level `fallbacks` wire key on /anthropic/v1/messages.
Two unrelated features share the key and are disambiguated by element shape:
  - array of STRINGS  ("provider/model") = Bifrost cross-provider gateway fallback.
  - array of OBJECTS  ([{"model": "..."}]) = Anthropic native server-side fallback
    (beta server-side-fallback-2026-06-01, refusal fallback).

Regressions pinned:
  1. Object-form fallbacks used to fail Bifrost-side unmarshalling with 'Invalid JSON'
     because AnthropicMessageRequest.Fallbacks was typed []string. It is now a shape
     tolerant union, so both forms parse.
  2. Native (object) fallbacks are Anthropic-only. Forwarding them to Vertex, Bedrock,
     Bedrock Mantle or Azure produced a live 400 'fallbacks: Extra inputs are not
     permitted', because AddMissingBetaHeadersToContext correctly withholds the beta
     header there while the field was still forwarded. Bifrost now strips native
     entries fail-closed for providers whose ProviderFeatures.ServerSideFallback is
     false. Note bedrock_mantle is NOT supported: it is Bedrock's native Anthropic
     surface, but feature availability still follows Amazon Bedrock.
  3. Bifrost string-form fallbacks must keep working and must never leak to a provider.

Both the Claude Code passthrough path (claude-cli User-Agent -> raw request body) and
the normalized path are covered, since the strip runs on the shared egress tail.

Not covered here: the `fallback` content block and stop_reason 'refusal'. Those only
occur when a safety classifier actually declines a request, which is non-deterministic
and inappropriate to provoke in a live sweep; they are covered by Go unit tests in
core/providers/anthropic/serversidefallback_test.go.

item.name: 27. Anthropic Fallback Credit Strip + Fallback Entry Fields
item.description:
Fallback credit (fallback-credit-2026-06-01; -2026-06-09 on the AWS surfaces) is a different feature from server-side fallback and has the INVERSE support matrix: the fallbacks parameter is Claude-API-only, while fallback_credit_token is supported nearly everywhere. A fallback entry also accepts output_config and speed, which were unmodelled and therefore dropped on the typed path.

SCOPE NOTE: this folder covers only what a 2xx-asserting suite can observe. Proving that fallback_credit_token is FORWARDED needs a token the platform will evaluate, and an invalid one is rejected with a 400 by design - which the collection-level "Status code is 2xx" test treats as a failure. Forwarding, the per-provider beta gating and the AWS beta-date rewrite are therefore covered by unit tests in core/providers/anthropic instead. Response-side shapes (the fallback content block and its trigger, stop_details.fallback_credit_token / fallback_has_prefill_claim) are also unit-tested: reproducing them here would require provoking a safety classifier, which is non-deterministic and inappropriate for a shared suite.

item.name: 28. Bedrock reasoning double-emission on additionalModelRequestFields (#5108 / PR #5109)
item.description: Regression for the #5108 follow-on fixed in PR #5109. A reasoning key (reasoning_config / thinking / Nova reasoningConfig) consumed into Params.Reasoning on Bedrock Converse ingress was ALSO forwarded verbatim via additionalModelRequestFieldPaths. The Bedrock egress then carried two copies (the re-synthesized thinking plus the passthrough reasoning_config, which Bedrock expands into thinking), and Converse rejected the collision: "The additional field thinking/type conflicts with an existing field". The fix strips the consumed reasoning key from the verbatim passthrough so exactly one reasoning field reaches Bedrock. Cases 1-3 pin the integration route (/bedrock converse) that actually reproduced the bug; case 4 is a native /v1 control (the native path has no passthrough, so it never collided) guarding the invariant from that entry point.

exec: // Regression #5108 / PR #5109. Exact user repro: client sends reasoning_config inside
// additionalModelRequestFields on the /bedrock Converse route. Before the fix the egress
// forwarded reasoning_config verbatim alongside the emitted thinking -> Converse collision.
pm.test('no thinking/reasoning double-emission collision on converse egress - #5108', function () {
pm.expect(t, 'Bedrock rejected a duplicated reasoning field: ' + t).to.not.match(/conflicts with an existing field/i);
pm.expect(t, 'Bedrock asked to remove a duplicated thinking field: ' + t).to.not.match(/Remove thinking\//i);
// Regression #5108 / PR #5109 (streaming). The collision is request-validation, so it
// surfaces as a synchronous 400 even on converse-stream. Assert it does not occur.
pm.test('no thinking/reasoning double-emission collision on converse-stream egress - #5108', function () {
pm.expect(t, 'Bedrock rejected a duplicated reasoning field: ' + t).to.not.match(/conflicts with an existing field/i);
pm.expect(t, 'Bedrock asked to remove a duplicated thinking field: ' + t).to.not.match(/Remove thinking\//i);
